text: This article provides a hermeneutical exploration of the diverse historical contexts surrounding Question and Answer 45 of the Heidelberg Catechism, centering on the resurrection of Jesus Christ. Beyond merely identifying the intended theological meaning of the text, this study seeks to uncover the underlying hermeneutical frameworks that shape its interpretation. Furthermore, it addresses the tension between scholarly analysis and the lived experience of lay Christians, who often perceive these specialized interpretive methods as foreign or unconventional. Ultimately, the paper demonstrates how divergent hermeneutical processes can lead to vastly different understandings of the same foundational doctrine—outcomes that are, at times, worlds apart.
